perturbation.original_text: Affiliate of the Party and/or any person specified in this A greement, means: (i) its parent company, its founder (participant or shareholder), including a physical person; and/or (ii) any company directly or indirectly controlled by the Party and/or its parent company.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate of the Party and/or any person specified in this A greement, means only its parent company, excluding its founder (participant or shareholder), including a physical person; and excluding any company directly or indirectly controlled by the Party and/or its parent company.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by redefining 'Affiliate' to explicitly exclude founders and indirectly controlled companies, conflicting with later mentions of 'Affiliates' rights and obligations, which would now be interpreted much more narrowly. This creates confusion about which entities are covered by the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS: I. The Disclosing Party is an [advisor to a potential buyer] who is willing to, either itself or through its Affiliate(s) (as defined below), acquire, directly or indirectly, shares in the Target (as defined bel ow) or assets owned by the Target (the “ Transaction ”).
perturbation.justification: The original text broadly defines 'Affiliate' to include parent companies, founders, and indirectly controlled companies. The changed text narrows this definition, explicitly excluding founders and indirectly controlled companies. The contradicted text refers to obligations of 'Affiliates' in the context of a transaction. Because the changed text excludes founders and indirectly controlled companies from the definition of 'Affiliate', the contradicted text now has a narrower scope, creating an in-text contradiction regarding which entities' actions are covered in the Transaction described, therefore any actions from the founder of the party will not be considered actions of the Affiliate, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information means any information and documents (whether or no t designated as confidential), including, without limitation, finan cial, tax, commercial, corporate, legal, technical, operational, management, business and other informa tion and documents concerning the Target or any of their Affiliates, as well as any and all informatio n and documents regarding the intended sale of the Target, that is directly or indirectly disclosed, f urnished or made available, whether before or after the date hereof, in any way or by any means whatsoever (including, without limitation, in writing, orally or in electronic form), in a data room or elsewhere, to t he Receiving Party or any employees of the Receivin g Party, by any director, manager, agent, representat ive, advisor or employee of the Disclosing Party or any Affiliate of the Disclosing Party, in connection wi th the intended sale of the Target.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information means any information and documents (whether or no t designated as confidential), including, without limitation, finan cial, tax, commercial, corporate, legal, technical information and documents concerning the Target, as well as any and all informatio n and documents regarding the intended sale of the Target, that is directly or indirectly disclosed, f urnished or made available, whether before or after the date hereof, in any way or by any means whatsoever (including, without limitation, in writing, orally or in electronic form), in a data room or elsewhere, to t he Receiving Party or any employees of the Receivin g Party, by any director, manager, agent, representat ive, advisor or employee of the Disclosing Party, in connection wi th the intended sale of the Target. Confidential information only pertains to technical data.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that confidential information only pertains to technical data. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the definition that includes 'financial, tax, commercial, corporate, legal' information, creating uncertainty about what information is truly protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information means any information and documents (whether or no t designated as confidential), including, without limitation, finan cial, tax, commercial, corporate, legal, technical information and documents concerning the Target, as well as any and all informatio n and documents regarding the intended sale of the Target, that is directly or indirectly disclosed, f urnished or made available, whether before or after the date hereof, in any way or by any means whatsoever (including, without limitation, in writing, orally or in electronic form), in a data room or elsewhere, to t he Receiving Party or any employees of the Receivin g Party, by any director, manager, agent, representat ive, advisor or employee of the Disclosing Party, in connection wi th the intended sale of the Target. Confidential information only pertains to technical data.
perturbation.justification: The changed text includes the sentence "Confidential information only pertains to technical data." This directly contradicts the preceding definition which includes 'financial, tax, commercial, corporate, legal, technical information'. This is a clear and direct conflict in the document creating an inconsistency about what types of information are considered confidential. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the addition of the last sentance contradicts the first part of the definition, leading to an 'Inconsistency - In Text Contradiction', as defined by the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party undertakes not to disclose Conf idential Information to other persons, except the employees of the Receiving Party listed in Anne x 1 to this Agreement that need to possess such information in order to achieve the purposes s pecified in Section 2 of this Agreement and who have signed confidentiality agreements or are o therwise obliged to maintain confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information to the extent specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party undertakes to disclose Conf idential Information to other persons, including the employees of the Receiving Party not listed in Anne x 1 to this Agreement that need to possess such information in order to achieve the purposes s pecified in Section 2 of this Agreement and who have not signed confidentiality agreements and are not obliged to maintain confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information to the extent specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'not to disclose' to 'to disclose', this creates a direct in-text contradiction with the overall intent of the NDA, which is to prevent disclosure. This undermines the core purpose of the agreement and creates a significant legal ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The subject of this Agreement is to secure the conf identiality of information determined hereunder as Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text prevents disclosure of confidential information while the changed text mandates disclosure. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states the agreement's purpose is to secure confidentiality. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the agreement now both requires and forbids disclosure of confidential information, undermining its core purpose, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the preservation and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information under this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect without being limited in time, except for cases when the Disclosi ng Party provides its written consent as to any reduction of said term.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the preservation and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information under this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect limited to one day after the contract is initiated, except for cases when the Disclosi ng Party provides its written consent as to any reduction of said term.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the duration of the confidentiality obligations to one day, this directly contradicts the original statement of 'without being limited in time' and undermines the long-term protection of confidential information that is typically expected in an NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the preservation and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information under this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect limited to one day after the contract is initiated, except for cases when the Disclosi ng Party provides its written consent as to any reduction of said term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the confidentiality obligations to one day, directly contradicting the implied intention of the original text of having confidentiality obligations remain in full force 'without being limited in time'. The contradicted text is the changed text, which means it causes a contradiction against itself and thus the contract in which it exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall enter into effect upon its exe cution and shall remain effective for three (3) years, except for the obligations of the Receiving Party as to preservation and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information set forth in Section 5 hereof above which remain in force after termination (or expiration) of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall not enter into effect upon its exe cution and shall remain not effective for three (3) years, except for the obligations of the Receiving Party as to preservation and nondisclosure of the Confidential Information set forth in Section 5 hereof above which remain in force after termination (or expiration) of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the agreement does not enter into effect upon execution, it directly contradicts the standard legal practice and expectation that a contract becomes binding when signed. This also contradicts the later sections that assume the agreement is in effect and enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be binding for both Parties. T his Agreement is executed in favor of the Parties and their respective successors. Neither Pa rty shall have the right to assign or otherwise transfer its rights or obligations under this Agree ment to other persons except with the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement does 'not enter into effect' upon execution and 'remain not effective' for three years. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'This Agreement shall be binding for both Parties.' If the agreement is not in effect, it cannot be binding. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where the changed text creates a conflict with the contradicted section making the terms unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, t he Receiving Party undertakes to promptly return to the Disclosing Party, or, at the Disclosi ng Party’s discretion, destroy all the Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limitation, on c omputer disks and/or other digital media). Within five (5) calendar days from the date of term ination (expiration) of this Agreement, unless there was a written request from the Disclosing Par ty, the Receiving Party shall destroy all Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limit ation, on computer disks and/or other digital media). In case of destruction of Confidential Info rmation under the terms of this Section, the Receiving Party shall send to the Disclosing Party a written confirmation of such destruction, which should contain a list of the destroyed Confid ential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, t he Receiving Party is not obligated to return to the Disclosing Party, or, at the Disclosi ng Party’s discretion, destroy all the Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limitation, on c omputer disks and/or other digital media). Within five (5) calendar days from the date of term ination (expiration) of this Agreement, unless there was a written request from the Disclosing Par ty, the Receiving Party shall not destroy all Confidential Information (together with all copies and extracts) stored by the Receiving Party and/or its representatives (including without limit ation, on computer disks and/or other digital media). In case of destruction of Confidential Info rmation under the terms of this Section, the Receiving Party shall not send to the Disclosing Party a written confirmation of such destruction, which should contain a list of the destroyed Confid ential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the obligations to return or destroy confidential information and to provide written confirmation, this directly undermines the ability of the Disclosing Party to control its confidential information after the agreement, creating a significant gap in protection. This contradicts the overall purpose of the NDA to keep the information confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The subject of this Agreement is to secure the conf identiality of information determined hereunder as Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Receiving Party is 'not obligated' to return or destroy confidential information, and will 'not send' written confirmation of destruction, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that specifies the subject of the agreement is to 'secure the confidentiality of information'. The change undermines the agreement's purpose, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies' definition where conflicting obligations are introduced.
